samplePackages.name: Comprehensive Code Review
samplePackages.description: Multi-faceted code review covering security, performance, correctness, and best practices
samplePackages.author: agora-community

samplePackages.name: API Design Review
samplePackages.description: Review REST/GraphQL API designs for consistency, scalability, and developer experience
samplePackages.author: agora-community

samplePackages.name: Refactoring Strategy
samplePackages.description: Plan and execute safe, incremental codebase refactoring with test coverage
samplePackages.author: agora-community

samplePackages.name: Structured Debugging
samplePackages.description: Systematic root-cause analysis — form hypotheses, gather evidence, identify the bug
samplePackages.author: agora-community

samplePackages.name: Test Strategy Planner
samplePackages.description: Design a comprehensive testing strategy covering unit, integration, e2e, and property-based tests
samplePackages.author: agora-community

samplePackages.name: Migration Plan Generator
samplePackages.description: Plan safe, phased migrations — framework upgrades, database migrations, cloud provider moves
samplePackages.author: agora-community

sampleWorkflows.name: TDD Development Cycle
sampleWorkflows.description: Test-driven development — write failing test, implement, refactor. Repeat.
sampleWorkflows.author: testdriven
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
You are following TDD methodology. For each feature request:
1. Write a failing test first
2. Write minimal code to pass the test
3. Refactor while keeping tests green

Always start by understanding the requirements and writing tests that describe expected behavior.

sampleWorkflows.name: Security Audit Workflow
sampleWorkflows.description: Thorough security analysis covering OWASP Top 10, secrets, and dependency vulnerabilities
sampleWorkflows.author: security-first
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Perform a thorough security audit of the codebase:
1. Check for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities
2. Look for hardcoded secrets and credentials
3. Validate input sanitization
4. Check dependency vulnerabilities
5. Review authentication and authorization
6. Verify CSRF and XSS protections

Report findings with severity levels (Critical/High/Medium/Low) and suggested fixes.

sampleWorkflows.name: API Design Review
sampleWorkflows.description: Review API endpoints for RESTful best practices, consistency, and developer experience
sampleWorkflows.author: api-craft
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Review the API design against these criteria:
1. RESTful resource naming consistency
2. HTTP method usage correctness
3. Request/response structure
4. Error handling and status codes
5. Authentication and authorization
6. Pagination, filtering, and sorting patterns
7. Rate limiting documentation
8. Versioning strategy

Provide specific recommendations for each issue found.

sampleWorkflows.name: Large Scale Refactor
sampleWorkflows.description: Safe, incremental refactoring strategy for large codebases with minimal risk
sampleWorkflows.author: agora-community
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Plan and execute a large-scale refactor:
1. Map the current architecture and dependencies
2. Identify refactoring targets by risk level
3. Break the refactor into small, reversible steps
4. Ensure test coverage before each change
5. Execute changes incrementally
6. Verify no regressions after each step
7. Update documentation

Always prioritize keeping the system working over speed of refactoring.

sampleWorkflows.name: Database Migration Review
sampleWorkflows.description: Review SQL migrations for safety, performance, and rollback readiness
sampleWorkflows.author: dba-master
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Review the database migration:
1. Check for backward compatibility
2. Verify index usage and query performance
3. Ensure rollback script exists and works
4. Check data type correctness
5. Validate constraint and foreign key handling
6. Review for locking concerns
7. Confirm zero-downtime deploy compatibility

Flag any migration that could cause data loss or extended downtime.

sampleWorkflows.name: Architecture Review
sampleWorkflows.description: High-level architecture review focusing on modularity, coupling, and scalability
sampleWorkflows.author: arch-wizard
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Review the architecture:
1. Evaluate module boundaries and separation of concerns
2. Check for circular dependencies
3. Review error propagation patterns
4. Assess scalability of the design
5. Verify logging and observability
6. Check configuration management
7. Review testing strategy completeness

Provide a score (1-10) for each dimension with actionable improvements.

sampleWorkflows.name: Documentation Generator
sampleWorkflows.description: Generate comprehensive documentation for codebases — API docs, README, and inline comments
sampleWorkflows.author: doc-master
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Generate thorough documentation for the codebase:
1. Create or update README with setup, usage, and architecture
2. Document all public APIs with parameter descriptions
3. Add inline code comments for complex logic
4. Generate API reference docs from TypeScript types
5. Create changelog from git history
6. Add contributing guide
7. Document environment variables and configuration

Focus on clarity and completeness. The docs should help new contributors onboard quickly.

sampleWorkflows.name: Incident Post-Mortem
sampleWorkflows.description: Structured incident analysis — timeline, root cause, impact, and action items
sampleWorkflows.author: sre-team
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Conduct a blameless post-mortem analysis:
1. Establish incident timeline from logs and metrics
2. Identify root cause and contributing factors
3. Document impact (users affected, downtime duration, data loss)
4. Analyze detection and response time
5. Identify systemic issues beyond the immediate cause
6. Propose specific action items with owners
7. Define monitoring to detect recurrence

The goal is learning, not blame. Every action item should be specific and testable.

sampleWorkflows.name: Dependency Audit
sampleWorkflows.description: Audit project dependencies for security, licensing, and maintenance status
sampleWorkflows.author: safety-first
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Audit all project dependencies:
1. Check for known CVEs in each dependency
2. Review license compatibility with your project
3. Evaluate maintenance activity (last commit, release cadence)
4. Identify unused or duplicate dependencies
5. Check for deprecated packages
6. Verify lockfile integrity
7. Suggest alternatives for problematic dependencies

Priority order: security issues first, licensing second, maintenance third.

sampleWorkflows.name: Performance Audit
sampleWorkflows.description: Identify and fix performance bottlenecks in code and database queries
sampleWorkflows.author: perf-nerd
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Conduct a performance audit:
1. Identify N+1 query patterns
2. Review algorithm complexity (Big O)
3. Check for memory leaks
4. Review caching strategy
5. Analyze bundle size and tree-shaking
6. Check lazy-loading opportunities
7. Review database query performance with EXPLAIN ANALYZE

Quantify improvements where possible (e.g., "reduces page load from 3s to 200ms").

sampleWorkflows.name: CI/CD Pipeline Review
sampleWorkflows.description: Review CI/CD configuration for speed, reliability, and security best practices
sampleWorkflows.author: devops-pro
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Review the CI/CD pipeline:
1. Check build caching effectiveness
2. Verify test parallelization
3. Review deployment stages and gates
4. Check secret management
5. Verify rollback procedures
6. Review notification and alerting
7. Check pipeline security (supply chain)

Suggest specific config changes to reduce pipeline time and improve reliability.

sampleWorkflows.name: New Project Scaffold
sampleWorkflows.description: Scaffold a new project with best-practice structure, tooling, and configuration
sampleWorkflows.author: agora-community
sampleWorkflows.prompt:
Scaffold a new project:
1. Set up the project structure following community best practices
2. Configure TypeScript/linting/formatting
3. Set up testing framework
4. Configure CI/CD
5. Add editor config and git hooks
6. Create README with badges
7. Set up dependency management
8. Add Docker support if applicable

Ask about the project type first, then generate a complete scaffold.

sampleTutorials.title: MCP Servers 101
sampleTutorials.description: Learn the fundamentals of Model Context Protocol and how to use it with OpenCode

steps.title: What is MCP?
steps.content: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard that lets AI models connect with external tools and data sources. Think of it as a USB-C port for AI — a universal way to plug any AI into any tool. MCP was created by Anthropic and is now governed by the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation.

steps.title: Installing Your First MCP Server
steps.content: Let's install the filesystem MCP server. OpenCode uses MCP servers to access your files, run commands, and interact with APIs.

steps.title: Configure in OpenCode
steps.content: Add the MCP server to your OpenCode configuration and restart opencode to pick it up:

steps.title: Test It Out
steps.content: Now in OpenCode, try asking: "List the files in the current directory" or "Read the package.json file". The AI will use the filesystem MCP server to access your files safely.

sampleTutorials.title: OpenCode Agents & Skills Deep Dive
sampleTutorials.description: Master the three-tier extensibility system: Skills, Agents, and Plugins

steps.title: Skills — Lightweight Prompts
steps.content: Skills are markdown files with frontmatter that define agent behavior. They're the lightest form of customization. Create a file called `.opencode/skills/reviewer.md` and OpenCode will load it automatically.
steps.code:
---
name: code-reviewer
description: Reviews code for quality and security
---

You are a senior code reviewer. Focus on:
- Security vulnerabilities
- Performance issues
- Code style and maintainability

steps.title: Agents — Specialized Workers
steps.content: Agents are specialized workers with their own system prompt and tool permissions. You can define them in your opencode.json or using the `opencode agent create` command. Each agent can have different model, permissions, and behavior.

steps.title: Plugins — Full Integration
steps.content: Plugins are npm packages that provide full TypeScript integration with hooks, tools, and event handlers. Agora itself runs as a plugin. Plugins can add custom tools, respond to events, and integrate deeply with OpenCode.

sampleTutorials.title: TDD with OpenCode
sampleTutorials.description: Use the TDD workflow to drive test-first development with AI assistance

steps.title: Install the TDD Workflow
steps.content: Agora comes with a TDD workflow. View it first:

steps.title: Apply the Workflow
steps.content: Apply the workflow as an OpenCode skill to use it during development:

steps.title: Start Coding
steps.content: Now when you ask OpenCode to implement a feature, it will automatically follow TDD — write the test first, implement, then refactor. The structured thinking MCP server helps with the planning phase.

sampleTutorials.title: Security Auditing with OpenCode
sampleTutorials.description: Run automated security audits using the security workflow and community tools

steps.title: Install Security Tools
steps.content: First, install the security audit workflow and the necessary MCP servers:
steps.code:
agora use wf-security-audit
agora install mcp-github

steps.title: Run an Audit
steps.content: In OpenCode, use the security workflow: "Run a security audit on this project". The agent will check for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, hardcoded secrets, dependency vulnerabilities, and more.

steps.title: Review Findings
steps.content: The audit produces a prioritized report with severity levels and suggested fixes. Each finding includes a clear remediation step you can ask OpenCode to implement.

sampleTutorials.title: Building Custom MCP Servers
sampleTutorials.description: Create your own MCP servers using the TypeScript SDK

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.content: Make sure you have the MCP SDK installed and a basic understanding of TypeScript.

steps.title: Create a Basic Server
steps.content: An MCP server exposes tools, resources, and prompts. Here is a minimal server with one tool:

steps.title: Test Your Server
steps.content: Run it and test with an MCP inspector or add it to OpenCode:

sampleTutorials.title: Docker Development with MCP
sampleTutorials.description: Manage Docker containers, images, and compose files from your AI assistant

steps.title: Install the Docker MCP Server
steps.content: First, install the Docker MCP server globally. This lets your AI assistant manage containers.

steps.title: Configure in OpenCode
steps.content: Add the Docker MCP server to your opencode.json:

steps.title: Common Docker Tasks
steps.content: Now you can ask your AI to: list running containers, view logs, restart services, build images, manage Docker Compose, and clean up unused resources — all from your terminal via natural language.

sampleTutorials.title: API Testing with MCP Tools
sampleTutorials.description: Learn to test and debug APIs using MCP servers and OpenCode workflows

steps.title: Set Up API Testing Tools
steps.content: Install the necessary MCP servers for API testing. The Brave Search and GitHub servers are useful for testing external APIs.

steps.title: Test REST Endpoints
steps.content: With the MCP tools configured, you can ask OpenCode to test API endpoints: "Make a GET request to https://api.example.com/health" or "Test the POST /users endpoint with this payload."

steps.title: Automate API Tests
steps.content: Use the TDD workflow combined with API tools to write automated tests: "Create a test suite that verifies all API endpoints return correct status codes and response shapes."

sampleTutorials.title: Vector Search with MCP
sampleTutorials.description: Set up and query vector databases for semantic search and RAG applications

steps.title: Choose a Vector Database
steps.content: Agora supports several vector databases. For local development, ChromaDB is the easiest to start with. For production, Pinecone or Qdrant are recommended.
steps.code:
agora search vector
agora browse mcp-chromadb

steps.title: Install and Configure
steps.content: Install your chosen vector database MCP server and add it to OpenCode:

steps.title: Semantic Search in Action
steps.content: With vector search configured, you can ask OpenCode to: "Search for documents similar to this text" or "Find the most relevant code examples for implementing OAuth." The AI will use the vector database to find semantically similar content.

sampleTutorials.title: OpenCode Power User Tips
sampleTutorials.description: Advanced tips and workflows for getting the most out of OpenCode

steps.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
steps.content: Learn the essential keybindings: Tab switches between agents, Ctrl+C interrupts the current action, Ctrl+L clears the conversation, / lists all available commands.

steps.title: Custom Agents
steps.content: Create specialized agents for different tasks. For example, a "reviewer" agent with read-only permissions for code review, or a "deploy" agent with access to deployment tools.
steps.code: opencode agent create --name reviewer --mode primary --permissions read,glob,grep

steps.title: Session Management
steps.content: Continue previous sessions with `opencode -c`, fork them with `opencode --fork`, or start a specific agent with `opencode --agent reviewer`. Sessions persist across restarts.

sampleTutorials.title: Understand the Trust Gate
sampleTutorials.description: Read Agora gate output correctly before installing an MCP server

steps.title: What the Gate Means
steps.content: Agora's live gate checks known red flags before a write: description-injection patterns, declared permissions, registry status, package reachability, and drift when a probe baseline exists. A clean result means no known red flags, not safe.

steps.title: Preview Before Writing
steps.content: Use `agora acquire <id-or-query> --dry-run --json` to resolve a package, build the install plan, and inspect the gate verdict without changing any host config.

steps.title: Handle Warnings
steps.content: Warnings require explicit acceptance. Read the checks first; only re-run with `--accept-warnings` when the warning is acceptable for the project.

steps.title: Treat Failures as Stop Signs
steps.content: A hard fail blocks the write and exits non-zero. Do not report a passing gate as a safety guarantee, and do not look for bypass flags around failed checks.

sampleTutorials.title: Contributing to the Catalog
sampleTutorials.description: Add MCP servers, workflows, and tutorials to the Agora ecosystem

steps.title: The Offline Catalog
steps.content: Agora ships with a bundled offline catalog in src/data.ts. It contains MCP servers, prompt tools, workflows, and tutorials. The catalog is refreshed periodically and serves as the default data source when no API is available.

steps.title: Add an MCP Server
steps.content: To add a new MCP server, append an entry to the samplePackages array in src/data.ts. Include the id, name, npm package, description, tags, and metadata. Run the tests to verify your entry works.
steps.code:
{
  id: 'mcp-my-server',
  name: '@you/mcp-server',
  description: 'Description of what it does',
  author: 'You',
  version: '1.0.0',
  category: 'mcp',
  tags: ['your-tag', 'tool'],
  stars: 100,
  installs: 500,
  npmPackage: '@you/mcp-server',
  createdAt: '2026-05-15'
}

steps.title: Add a Workflow
steps.content: Workflows are structured prompts that guide AI behavior. Add them to the sampleWorkflows array with a descriptive prompt that includes step-by-step instructions, tags, and metadata.
steps.code:
{
  id: 'wf-my-workflow',
  name: 'My Workflow Name',
  description: 'What this workflow does',
  author: 'you',
  prompt: 'Step-by-step instructions for the AI to follow...',
  tags: ['your-tag'],
  stars: 1,
  forks: 0,
  createdAt: '2026-05-15'
}

steps.title: Add a Tutorial
steps.content: Tutorials guide users through multi-step learning paths. Add them to the sampleTutorials array with a unique id, title, level, duration, and an array of steps. Each step has a title and content, with optional code blocks.

steps.title: Submit a Pull Request
steps.content: After adding your entries to data.ts, run `bun run test`, `bun run typecheck`, and `bun run build`, then submit a PR on GitHub. See CONTRIBUTING.md for the full contribution guide.

sampleTutorials.title: Verify an Agora Lockfile
sampleTutorials.description: Use agora.lock and the local store to catch manifest drift

steps.title: Know the Split
steps.content: `agora.toml` is human intent. `agora.lock` is machine truth: exact artifact identity, hashes, policy verdicts, and where the artifact is synced.

steps.title: Verify the Lock
steps.content: Run `agora lock verify` from a project with an `agora.lock`. The command compares the lockfile's approved hashes against the current manifest in the local SQLite store.

steps.title: Read Drift Output
steps.content: Any manifest or per-tool hash mismatch exits 1 and prints the changed artifact. Treat that as a quarantine signal until the diff is reviewed.

steps.title: Use a Custom Store in CI
steps.content: Automation can point verification at an isolated SQLite store with `--store <path>` or `AGORA_DB_PATH`.
